rulings.Case Title: SEARS, ROEBUCK AND CO. vs. WRABLEWSKI, ROBBIN D et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft'S Motion To Vacate The Renewal Of Judgment Entered On Jul-18-2011
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT WRABLEWSKI, ROBBIN D Notice Of Defendant's Motion To Vacate The Renewal Of Judgment Entered On Jul-18-2011 Granted. No opposition filed. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's motion is timely under CCP § 683.170(b), as it was filed within 30 days of service of the notice of renewal on October 24, 2013. Following Fidelity Creditor Service, Inc. v. Browne (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 195, 203-204, the trial court can vacate a renewed judgment based on failure to serve the summons and complaint properly. The unopposed declaration of Ms. Wrablewski demonstrates that the 2192 Diamond Street was not her residence or usual place of abode as of June, 2001. (See Declaration of Wrablewski, 2-4.) Consequently, substituted service was not properly completed under CCP § 415.20(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY, A MASSACHUSETTS VS. ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For A Determination That Deft/Cross-Deft California Drywall Co.'S Settlement Is Not In Good Faith Under Code Civ Proc Sec 877.6
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY, INC.'S Motion For A Determination That Defendant/Cross-Defendant California Drywall Co.'s Settlement Is Not In Good Faith Under Code Civ Proc Sec 877.6 Denied. California Drywall Co.'s application for determination of good faith settlement meets the requirements of CCP § 877.6(a)(2). Defendant/Cross-Complainant Anderson Row & Buckley, Inc. ("ARB") has not carried its burden of showing the settlement is "out of the ball park" as to Defendant/Cross-Complainant's California Drywall Co.'s proportional liability. (See Tech-Bilt v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488.) ARB's request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. YONG JOO LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Adjudication Granted. Plaintiff met its burden of producing evidence that Defendant breached his obligations to Plaintiff under the Equipment Financing Agreement. (See Declaration of Lee in Support of Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment and/or Summary Adjudication of Claim 1-7, Exhs. 1-3.) The burden shifted to Defendant. Defendant failed to meet his burden as no opposition to the motion for summary judgment was filed. Plaintiff is entitled to judgment in its favor and against Defendant Yong Joo Lee in the amount of $161,945.34. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: IRENE HSIEH VS. SOLAR POWER INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses, For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 1: PLAINTIFF IRENE HSIEH's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff IRENE HSIEH's Motion is DENIED in its entirety. Inasmuch as the defamation claim has been adjudicated in favor of the defendants, those requests are moot. Plaintiff has not shown that there has been a specific lack of compliance with the prior discovery Order on September 5, 2013. In fact, it would appear that Plaintiff's refusal to more narrowly tailor their requests predictably resulted in a large production of documents. Plaintiff's request for an adverse inference and appointment of forensic expert are not briefed substantially enough and are better left to the trial court to decide. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrgoatories Propounded To Defts Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 2: PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrgoatories Propounded To Defendants; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is DENIED. The Special Interrogatories in question do not comply with CCP section 2030.60(d) as each one is not "full and complete in and of itself." Further, they require the answering party to refer to outside materials which is improper as set forth in Catanese v. Superior Court (1996) 46 CA4th 1159, 1164-1165. Finally, the specially prepared interrogatories, No. 1 in particular, are compound. (CCP section 2030.060(f)) Plaintiff was invited by counsel for defendants to propound additional interrogatories in the correct form, which he did, yet still pressed forward with the instant motion. Plaintiff is ORDERED to pay $750.00 by certified funds to counsel for defendant Miguel Alejandro Rescalvo as this motion was brought without substantial justification. (CCP section 2030.300(d)) Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT MIGUEL RESCALVO, DET NORSKE VERITAS AS, DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled in part and sustained in part, with 20 days leave to amend. The Court did not consider any extrinsic evidence in ruling on this motion, such as the Declaration of Mark Emmett Wilson and the attached exhibits. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, "a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable.") As to the demurrer on uncertainty grounds, the Third Amended Complaint adequately pleads that Plaintiff was employed by both corporate entities and makes his allegations against both of them. (See, e.g. TAC, 1, 64, 73, 87, 92, 103, 109, 115; see also Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616, holding that a demurrer for uncertainty is a disfavored ground and should be overruled if facts are presumptively with defendant's knowledge.) As to the demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud, the cause of action is properly pled. (See Order Overruling Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint dated May 14, 2013.) As to the demurrer to the sixth cause of action for employee misclassification, it is not preempted because the cause of action appears to seek "other related employment benefits" to remedy violations of legal duties independent of ERISA. (See TAC 113; Marin General Hospital v. Modesto & Empire Traction Co. (2009) 581 F.3d 941, 947.) As to the demurrer to the eighth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. See Shepppard v. Freeman (1998) 67 Cal. App. 4th 339, 349 ("employees, regardless of their scope of employment or personal motives, cannot be individually liable for their acts or words relating to personnel actions unless such liability arises from statute") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT MIGUEL RESCALVO, DET NORSKE VERITAS AS, DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted in part and denied in part. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The motion to strike the entire Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") is denied. The motion to strike the reference to a 401(k) is granted. The following language is stricken from the TAC: "paragraph 113, line 28, the words "lost 401(k) contributions and other." The motion to strike the prayer for attorney's fees is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mark Wilson'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 24, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff Mark Wilson'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is DENIED. The Form Interrogatories- Employment Law are not properly served on co-employees, but rather the employer as set forth in definitions found in section 4(e) of the Form Interrogatories-Employment Law. Sanctions are DENIED to both parties. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
